roadmap_text: Files Touched: crates/hippo-daemon/src/daemon.rs, claude_session.rs, native_messaging.rs
objection: native_messaging.rs does not belong in P0.2's file list. 01-source-health.md §Write Paths explicitly states: 'native_messaging.rs does NOT write directly to SQLite... The source_health update for browser therefore occurs in flush_events, not in native_messaging.rs.' 08-anti-patterns.md reinforces this.
evidence: grep -n 'rusqlite\|Connection' crates/hippo-daemon/src/native_messaging.rs returns 0 hits. The actual P0.2 branch (feat/p0.2-source-health-write-paths) does not modify native_messaging.rs. The roadmap row contradicts its own design doc.
suggested_fix: Drop native_messaging.rs from the Files Touched cell. List only daemon.rs (flush_events for shell/claude-tool/browser) and claude_session.rs (segment upsert).

roadmap_text: P0.2 Effort: ~4h
objection: 4h for P0.2 is roughly 3-5x too low. The actual in-flight P0.2 branch shows +325/-846 in daemon.rs, a new 220-line integration test (source_health_write_paths.rs), changes to claude_session.rs, storage.rs, config.rs, telemetry.rs, plus Grafana dashboard adjustments, plus six review-cycle fix commits for idle-tick semantics, Ok(-1) duplicate handling, partial-success tick gating, and recompute_rolling_counts shutdown/first-tick issues.
evidence: git diff main feat/p0.2-source-health-write-paths --stat; log of branch shows 5+ correction commits post-initial.
suggested_fix: Revise to ~1-1.5 days. Note recompute_rolling_counts, idle-tick success semantics, and duplicate-insert handling as sub-tasks that eat the long tail.

roadmap_text: P0.3 Effort: ~3h for checks 1, 4, 7, 8
objection: 3h for four doctor checks with tests understates scope. Check 1 alone must handle pre-migration (source_health missing) cleanly; Check 4 greps four zsh startup files and validates each reference; Check 7 walks the data dir; Check 8 handles the pre-P1.1 no-watchdog case. Plus ANSI color, fail_count aggregation, isolation via catch_unwind, and tests for each.
evidence: commands.rs grew to 2051 lines post-P0.3 with ~5 new fns (check_source_staleness, check_zsh_hook_sourced, check_log_file_sizes, check_watchdog_heartbeat). Per-check test scaffolding alone exceeds 3h.
suggested_fix: Revise to ~6-8h. Note the 'pre-migration DB' fallback path as a common sub-task shared across checks.

roadmap_text: P1.1 Scope: hippo watchdog subcommand + capture_alarms table + launchd plist + ack CLI + rate limiter
objection: Violates the guiding principle 'No PR touches more than one logical subsystem.' This single PR ships: (1) new subcommand + binary entry, (2) new table + migration, (3) new launchd agent + installer integration, (4) new user-facing CLI ('hippo alarms list/ack'), (5) rate-limit/dedup logic, (6) optional osascript notification path. Six subsystems.
evidence: Roadmap line 8: 'No PR touches more than one logical subsystem. Parallel worktrees avoid merge conflicts.'
suggested_fix: Split into P1.1a (watchdog daemon + capture_alarms table + rate limiter, gated behind config flag) and P1.1b (ack CLI + launchd install + notify path). Lets reviewers reason about policy separately from plumbing.

roadmap_text: P2.1 Effort: ~2-3 days
objection: Too aggressive. 06-claude-session-watcher.md commits to: notify-crate FSEvents integration (new dep), partial-line safety, inode/device truncation detection, per-file 30s timeout with backoff, dual-run parity instrumentation, unit + integration + end-to-end + property tests, plus NFS/iCloud detection. FSEvents coalescing under load is itself a debugging surface.
evidence: notify crate not in Cargo.toml (grep -rn 'notify' Cargo.toml returns 0). Property-testing with proptest is not in existing test suite.
suggested_fix: Revise to 4-6 days. Consider landing the watcher behind claude_session_mode='both' flag in a smaller PR first, then parity tooling + dual-run logging as a second PR.

roadmap_text: P0.4 Effort: ~2h — OTel source attribute
objection: 2h covers the code diff but misses the Grafana dashboard rename cost. The commit that shipped this (74916db) was specifically a rename type→source, which invalidates existing dashboard queries.
evidence: P0.2 branch includes otel/grafana/dashboards/hippo-daemon.json changes (+26/-26 lines) — dashboard realignment is shared with this PR.
suggested_fix: Bump to 3-4h and note the Grafana dashboard panels that need a coordinated label rename. Include Prometheus recording rules if any exist.

roadmap_text: Phase P1 starts after P0.1 AND P0.2 merge
objection: P1 is gated on the two longest-pole P0 PRs, and the roadmap's 'parallel agents' framing implies P1 work starts Day 2. If P0.2 is realistically 1-1.5 days (see A2), P1 slips by that much. Parallelism is bounded by the slowest P0 PR.
evidence: P0 parallelization claim assumes all 5 PRs merge same-day, which requires every P0 estimate to hold. A2 challenges that for P0.2.
suggested_fix: Either revise P0.2 estimate upward (consistent with A2) or explicitly list Day 2 as 'P0.2 finishing, P1.2 kicks off (only needs P0.1)' so stakeholders see the real critical path.

roadmap_text: M4: investigation issues #49–#53 closed with findings
objection: Depends on five specific GitHub issue numbers. Roadmap does not verify they exist or document their intended scope. If any are duplicates, closed, or scoped differently than the P3 agent assumes, M4 becomes ambiguous.
evidence: No cross-reference in 00-overview, 07-roadmap, or 08-anti-patterns mentions what #49-#53 cover.
suggested_fix: Inline a one-line summary of each issue (e.g., '#49 browser-dist regression; #50 tmux targeting') or replace with a pointer to a fixed docs/capture-reliability/investigation-index.md.

roadmap_text: P2.2 Verification: Row with probe_tag='synthetic' absent from hippo ask/hippo events; present in source_health
objection: Under-specifies the filter coverage. 05-synthetic-probes.md enumerates ~12 call sites needing WHERE probe_tag IS NULL (daemon commands.rs ×4, brain server.py ×2, brain enrichment.py, claude_sessions.py, browser_enrichment.py, mcp_queries.py ×4, rag.py, retrieval.py). A two-path spot-check is not adequate.
evidence: grep -rn 'probe_tag' brain/src/ returns 0 — zero brain-side filters exist yet; the full grep list in 05-synthetic-probes.md will be net-new.
suggested_fix: Strengthen verification to (a) a Rust+Python integration test enumerating every event-table SELECT and asserting exclusion, and (b) a CI linter/Semgrep rule that fails if a new SELECT from events/claude_sessions/browser_events lacks probe_tag IS NULL.

roadmap_text: P3.1 Blocks: P2.1 dual-run clean 48 h
objection: 'Clean' is undefined. 06-claude-session-watcher.md says 'watcher ≥ tailer, no misses' but doesn't say how no-misses is measured, by whom, or what tolerance counts as pass. This is a go/no-go criterion — it needs a predicate.
evidence: Phase A description in 06-claude-session-watcher.md §Migration Plan: 'compare row counts per path over 48 h. Exit criterion: watcher ≥ tailer, no misses.' Measurement mechanism absent.
suggested_fix: Define 'clean' concretely: a scheduled query like 'SELECT path, COUNT(*) FROM claude_sessions WHERE source_mode=tailer vs watcher and assert delta=0' run hourly; log divergences to a dedicated table. P3.1 reads that table before flipping default.

roadmap_text: Consensus review: P0.2 and P0.3 drafted against agreed schema; integrate after P0.1 merges
objection: Understates coupling. P0.3's Check 1 queries source_health, which requires the write contract of P0.2 (monotonic MAX on last_event_ts, consecutive_failures reset semantics). P0.3 is not 'drafted against schema' — it's drafted against P0.2's write contract.
evidence: commands.rs Check 1 and Check 8 both query source_health. Between P0.1 merge and P0.2 merge, Check 1 returns all-NULL last_event_ts — doctor must handle this gracefully (the shipped code does, see 'Pre-migration DB' fallback).
suggested_fix: Rephrase: 'P0.3 drafted against the source_health write contract in 01-source-health.md; Check 1 and Check 8 must gracefully handle the 0→rows-populated transition between P0.1 and P0.2 merges.'

roadmap_text: Watchdog: StartInterval=60 in launchd plist; I-7 threshold 180s = 3× interval
objection: launchd StartInterval schedules the next run N seconds after the previous *exit*, not every N seconds wall-clock. A 10s cycle under load drifts the cadence; the 3× tolerance (180s) collapses to 2× under drift.
evidence: 04-watchdog.md line 28: StartInterval=60. Apple launchd docs: StartInterval is time since last exit, not wall-clock interval.
suggested_fix: Either use LaunchAgent StartCalendarInterval for strict wall-clock, or (simpler) set I-7 threshold to 5× cadence (300s) to buy headroom. Document the launchd semantics in 04-watchdog.md.

roadmap_text: Check 8 — watchdog heartbeat reads source_health WHERE source='watchdog'
objection: Between P0.3 merge and P1.1 merge, this check has no backing data source. The shipped code returns gracefully (no row → pre-migration branch), but for users on post-P0 and pre-P1 builds Check 8 always reports 'no data' for 2-3 days of real wall-clock time.
evidence: commands.rs:1299 pre-migration fallback; no watchdog exists yet to populate the row.
suggested_fix: Add explicit release-note language for the P0 release: 'Check 8 shows [--] no data until watchdog ships in v0.X.' Or gate Check 8 behind a config flag that defaults off until P1.1.

roadmap_text: P0.3 covers checks 1, 4, 7, 8; Checks 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10 deferred to P1.3
objection: Check 3 (browser extension dist/ present) is the exact fingerprint of one of the two sev1s that motivated the overhaul ('Why this catches the sev1: This is precisely H2 — the exact regression would have been caught' per 03-doctor-upgrades.md). Deferring it to P1.3 (days 2-3) means the overhaul ships same-day but the motivating sev1 detection is 2-3 days out.
evidence: 03-doctor-upgrades.md Check 3: two stat() calls, <5 ms, trivial implementation.
suggested_fix: Move Check 3 into P0.3. Two stat calls adds 30 min to the P0.3 estimate. Keep the remaining checks in P1.3.

roadmap_text: P1.2 Scope: Extension heartbeat + NM host forwarding + probe_tag column stub + popup badge
objection: probe_tag column is already in the schema (shipped via P0.1, commit 96991b7). Listing a 'stub' in P1.2 is either redundant or refers to a different column — ambiguous.
evidence: git show 96991b7 --stat shows ALTER TABLE …ADD COLUMN probe_tag TEXT on three tables; crates/hippo-core/src/storage.rs:389-391 confirms.
suggested_fix: Remove 'probe_tag column stub' from P1.2 scope or rename it ('probe_tag populate logic' if P1.2 adds the first probe writer).

roadmap_text: Overhaul ships in four phases across ~7 days
objection: The roadmap's own math is: P0 day 1 + P1 days 2-3 + P2 days 4-7 + 48h dual-run + P3 cleanup → day 9-10, not day 7. 'Across ~7 days' risks giving stakeholders a wrong landing date.
evidence: Roadmap line 3 says 7 days; P2 ends day 7, then P3 gates on 48h dual-run.
suggested_fix: Restate as '~7 days for feature work, ~10 days to P3 cleanup and tmux-spawn removal.'

roadmap_text: Risk 'Watchdog alarm fatigue during bring-up': High likelihood, Medium impact, mitigation = notify_macos=false + conservative thresholds + opt-in notifications
objection: No rollback mechanism if it still fires too often during bring-up. Users who dismiss alarms 3x stop reading them — the same failure mode the overhaul is trying to prevent. Default alarm_rate_limit_minutes=15 is aggressive for a brand-new subsystem.
evidence: 04-watchdog.md §Configuration: alarm_rate_limit_minutes = 15 default.
suggested_fix: Ship P1.1 with alarm_rate_limit_minutes=60 for the first release (v0.17), step down to 15 in v0.18 after soak. Or add a 'bring-up mode' flag that suppresses alarms for the first 24 h after watchdog install.

roadmap_text: Risk 'probe_tag filter missing from a query path': Medium/Medium, mitigation = P2.2 integration test enumerating all query fns
objection: One integration test over ~12 call sites across two languages is fragile. A new query added months later has no guard rail.
evidence: 05-synthetic-probes.md §Grep List enumerates 12 files across daemon + brain + MCP.
suggested_fix: Add a Semgrep rule (aligns with repo .semgrep.yml) that matches SELECT ... FROM (events|claude_sessions|browser_events) without probe_tag IS NULL and fails CI. Pair with the integration test.

roadmap_text: 'No big-bang migrations'
objection: P0.1 is schema-level and strictly blocking — every downstream PR depends on it. Calling that 'not big-bang' is a phrasing stretch.
evidence: Roadmap line 16-17: 'P0.1 is the only blocking dependency' — itself a big-bang gate.
suggested_fix: Rephrase the principle as 'no big-bang feature migrations; schema-level migrations ship first as an atomic preamble.'

roadmap_text: M1: future source outage diagnosable via SELECT * FROM source_health in < 5 min without log-diving
objection: No drill, no measurement. 'Diagnosable in 5 min' is a human-timed metric that needs a scripted test.
evidence: 00-overview.md 'Success criteria #2': 5 min threshold not connected to a test.
suggested_fix: Add a M1 acceptance test: a scripted sev1 drill — disable Firefox → wait → run hippo doctor + SELECT source_health — stopwatch the time to root cause. Record in docs/capture-reliability/drills/.

roadmap_text: P0 estimates are per-agent (4h/4h/3h/2h/2h) with 'all five launch simultaneously'
objection: The team flow adds consensus review cycles (P0.2+P0.3 reviewed together post-P0.1 merge). Review cycles are not in the effort numbers. Real-world same-day merge of 5 PRs with consensus reviews is aggressive.
evidence: Roadmap §Team hippo-capture-p0 explicitly requires consensus review — but no cycle-time budget.
suggested_fix: Add a 'review + merge' line budget (e.g., 1h per PR) to the phase total. The 0.5-day P0 becomes 0.75-1 day realistically.

roadmap_text: Risk register omits: daemon.rs touched by multiple P0 PRs
objection: P0.2 modifies flush_events in daemon.rs; P0.4 adds OTel source attribute at call sites also inside flush_events (per the actual shipped rename). Both PRs touch the same function body. Worktree isolation doesn't prevent the merge conflict when both land.
evidence: git show 74916db — the OTel rename touched metrics call sites in flush paths.
suggested_fix: Add a merge-conflict row to the risk register; mitigation: merge P0.4 first (OTel rename with no schema changes), then P0.2 rebased on top.

roadmap_text: P1.3 Effort: ~6h for doctor checks 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10
objection: Six new checks in 6h is an hour per check. Check 5 (live-JSONL vs DB reconciliation) requires filesystem glob + N SQLite lookups + sessionId parsing. Check 6 requires tail -n 10000 + awk parse of session-hook-debug.log + SQL. Check 2 parses NM manifest JSON and stats the referenced binary.
evidence: 03-doctor-upgrades.md Check 5 perf budget <100 ms — but implementation complexity (glob + per-file readline + SQL) is not 1 hour.
suggested_fix: Revise to 1-1.5 days. Keep Check 2 and 3 in a 'quick-win' sub-PR (stat-only) and defer Check 5 + 6 to a second sub-PR with negative tests.

roadmap_text: P3.2: Delete tmux-spawn from shell/claude-session-hook.sh; reduce to marker write
objection: Deleting the tmux-spawn branch removes the backstop that kept sessions ingesting when the watcher was down between P2.1 and P3.1. AP-9 is strict about not removing fallbacks — tmux-spawn is not JSONL fallback but it is a recovery path.
evidence: 08-anti-patterns.md AP-9: 'Deleting the Fallback JSONL Path — Forbidden.' Different path but same reasoning applies.
suggested_fix: Before P3.2 merges, require 7-day clean window (not just 48h) and add a one-line 'revert instructions' section so operators can restore tmux-spawn behavior if the watcher regresses. Preserve hippo ingest claude-session --batch as the manual recovery.
